title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
author: Harold Abelson, Gerald Jay Sussman
excerpt: Teaches fundamental principles of computer programming using Scheme, covering recursion, abstraction, modularity, and programming language design. Used as MIT's introductory CS textbook from 1984 to 2007.
whoShouldRead: Readers interested in Computer Science and Programming and Education
keyIdeas: Central thesis of Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
keyTakeaways: Understand the core argument and its implications
